evals.name: eval-happy-path-de
evals.prompt: Ich möchte Trends für die Fertigungsindustrie scouten, Fokus Automobil und KI-gestützte Qualitätskontrolle
evals.expected_output: 60 trend candidates in German, organized across 4 dimensions x 3 horizons, with scoring metadata, saved to trend-scout-output.json and trend-candidates.md
expectations: trend-scout-output.json exists and is valid JSON
trend-scout-output.json contains exactly 60 candidates in tips_candidates.items
All 4 dimensions are present: externe-effekte, neue-horizonte, digitale-wertetreiber, digitales-fundament
All 3 horizons present per dimension: act, plan, observe
Each dimension-horizon cell has exactly 5 candidates
Each cell has at least 1 candidate per subcategory (3 subcategories per dimension)
Leading indicator ratio is at least 40%
All candidate scores are in valid range 0.0 to 1.0
trend-candidates.md exists and is written in German
tips-project.json exists with industry=Fertigung/manufacturing and subsector=Automobil/automotive
Project .metadata/ directory exists with trend-scout-output.json
Project .logs/ directory contains web-research-raw.json or phase1-research-summary.json
Project .logs/ directory contains trend-generator-candidates.json
trend-scout-output.json config.industry has bilingual labels (primary_en and primary_de)
trend-scout-output.json execution.workflow_state is 'agreed'
No duplicate trend names within the same dimension
At least 40% of web-signal candidates have source_url pointing to authoritative domains (gov, eu, org, edu, established industry/analyst sites)
Average source quality score across all candidates is at least 0.5 (CRAAP authority >= 3)
At least 40% of candidates are web-signal sourced (source_distribution.web_signal >= 24 out of 60)
Each candidate with source=web-signal has a non-empty source_url field
ACT candidates have signal_intensity 4-5, OBSERVE candidates have intensity 1-2 (horizon-intensity alignment)

evals.name: eval-happy-path-en
evals.prompt: Scout trends for the healthcare industry, digital health subsector, focusing on telemedicine adoption in DACH markets
evals.expected_output: 60 trend candidates in English, organized across 4 dimensions x 3 horizons, with scoring metadata, saved to trend-scout-output.json and trend-candidates.md
expectations: trend-scout-output.json exists and is valid JSON
trend-scout-output.json contains exactly 60 candidates in tips_candidates.items
All 4 dimensions are present: externe-effekte, neue-horizonte, digitale-wertetreiber, digitales-fundament
All 3 horizons present per dimension: act, plan, observe
Each dimension-horizon cell has exactly 5 candidates
Each cell has at least 1 candidate per subcategory (3 subcategories per dimension)
Leading indicator ratio is at least 40%
All candidate scores are in valid range 0.0 to 1.0
trend-candidates.md exists and is written in English
tips-project.json exists with industry=Healthcare and subsector=Digital Health
Project .metadata/ directory exists with trend-scout-output.json
Project .logs/ directory contains web-research-raw.json or phase1-research-summary.json
Project .logs/ directory contains trend-generator-candidates.json
trend-scout-output.json config.industry has bilingual labels (primary_en and primary_de)
trend-scout-output.json execution.workflow_state is 'agreed'
No duplicate trend names within the same dimension
At least 40% of web-signal candidates have source_url pointing to authoritative domains (gov, eu, org, edu, established industry/analyst sites)
Average source quality score across all candidates is at least 0.5 (CRAAP authority >= 3)
At least 40% of candidates are web-signal sourced (source_distribution.web_signal >= 24 out of 60)
Each candidate with source=web-signal has a non-empty source_url field
ACT candidates have signal_intensity 4-5, OBSERVE candidates have intensity 1-2 (horizon-intensity alignment)

evals.name: eval-review-de-automotive
evals.prompt: Ich möchte Trends für die Fertigungsindustrie scouten, Fokus Automobil und KI-gestützte Qualitätskontrolle
evals.expected_output: 60 reviewed trend candidates in German with stakeholder review verdicts, organized across 4 dimensions x 3 horizons
expectations: trend-scout-output.json exists and is valid JSON with 60 candidates
trend-candidates.md exists and is written in German
.metadata/candidate-review-verdicts/ directory exists with at least one verdict file
.metadata/candidate-review-verdicts/v1.json exists and is valid JSON
v1.json contains verdict field with value 'accept', 'revise', or 'reject'
v1.json contains stakeholder_reviews array with exactly 3 entries
v1.json stakeholder perspectives are: strategic_foresight_analyst, industry_domain_expert, downstream_pipeline_consumer
Each stakeholder review has a score (0-100) and criteria object with 5 scored criteria
Each criterion has score (pass/warn/fail), weight, and note fields
v1.json contains set_level_issues array (may be empty if no issues found)
v1.json contains revision_guidance object with action, priorities, and repair instructions
If verdict is 'revise', a v2.json exists after repair with final verdict 'accept'
trend-scout-output.json execution block contains candidate_review metadata
candidate_review metadata includes iterations count, final_verdict, and final_score
candidate_review.forced_accept is false when final_verdict is 'accept' with score >= 85
No training-sourced candidates have composite score > 0.65 (scoring cap enforcement)
No duplicate trend names across the entire pool (distinctiveness enforced)
Each dimension has candidates from at least 2 different subcategories (blind spot prevention)
All 60 candidates in final trend-candidates.md are present (no candidates lost during repair)
phases_completed in execution state includes 'phase-2.5'

evals.name: eval-review-en-healthcare
evals.prompt: Scout trends for the healthcare industry, digital health subsector, focusing on telemedicine adoption in DACH markets
evals.expected_output: 60 reviewed trend candidates in English with stakeholder review verdicts, bilingual review working correctly
expectations: trend-scout-output.json exists and is valid JSON with 60 candidates
trend-candidates.md exists and is written in English
.metadata/candidate-review-verdicts/v1.json exists and is valid JSON
v1.json verdict is 'accept', 'revise', or 'reject'
v1.json contains 3 stakeholder reviews with scores
Industry domain expert perspective evaluates healthcare/digital-health specificity, not generic tech trends
DACH market relevance criterion is scored — healthcare candidates reference EU MDR, DiGA, ePA, or DACH health institutions
Downstream pipeline consumer perspective evaluates TIPS expandability for telemedicine trends
trend-scout-output.json execution.candidate_review exists with iterations and final_verdict
phases_completed includes 'phase-2.5'
If repairs were made, candidates-compact.json was regenerated (file modification timestamp after v1.json timestamp)

evals.name: eval-review-cycle-effectiveness
evals.prompt: Scout trends for the energy industry, renewable energy subsector, focusing on smart grid integration and energy storage in European markets. Use thorough research mode.
evals.expected_output: 60 reviewed trend candidates with comprehensive stakeholder review that identifies and addresses pool-level quality issues
expectations: trend-scout-output.json exists with 60 candidates
.metadata/candidate-review-verdicts/v1.json exists with complete 3-perspective review
Strategic foresight analyst perspective evaluates signal grounding (>= 45% web-sourced target)
Strategic foresight analyst evaluates leading indicator coverage (>= 40% target)
Industry domain expert evaluates energy/renewable-specific content — not generic sustainability trends
Industry domain expert checks for EU energy regulations (Clean Energy Package, RED III, grid codes)
Downstream pipeline consumer evaluates cross-dimension linkage potential for value-modeler
Set-level issues analysis identifies any duplicate clusters or coverage blind spots
If review found issues: repair was attempted and v2.json shows improvement over v1.json scores
Final candidate pool has no dimension where all 15 candidates share a single keyword
trend-scout-output.json execution.candidate_review.final_score >= 70
Research hints in final candidates are actionable (specific data sources, quantitative targets, named institutions)
